text:
COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT
FoundationOne CDx (Foundation Medicine, Inc.)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATIENT & SPECIMEN INFORMATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Patient Name:          Margaret Okonkwo
Date of Birth:         [on file]
Sex:                   Female
Ordering Physician:    [Medical Oncology / Genitourinary Oncology]
Diagnosis (Submitted): Renal cell carcinoma, clear cell type, right kidney —
                       Stage IV with regional lymphadenopathy and bilateral
                       pulmonary metastases

Specimen ID:           [BED5D566-RK-01]
Specimen Type:         CT-guided percutaneous core needle biopsy, right renal mass
Sample Type:           FFPE (formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded) tissue
Tumor Content (Pathologist-Estimated): 60%
Date of Procedure:     [see clinical record]
Date Received:         [see clinical record]
Date Report Issued:    [see clinical record]
Test Platform:         FoundationOne CDx (FDA-approved companion diagnostic)
Analysis Version:      CDx v6.1

Quality Control:       PASS
  — Median exon coverage: 742x
  — Sequencing uniformity (≥100x): 97.8%
  — Specimen quality adequate for full analysis

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GENOMIC OVERVIEW
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB):     5 mutations/Mb  [LOW; threshold for HIGH: ≥10 mut/Mb]
Microsatellite Status (MS):        Microsatellite Stable (MSS)
Genomic Loss of Heterozygosity:    HIGH — 23% of evaluable genomic segments
                                   (LOH High defined as ≥16% of genome; relevant
                                   to PARP inhibitor and platinum sensitivity
                                   biomarker contexts)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SHORT VARIANT ALTERATIONS — PATHOGENIC / LIKELY PATHOGENIC
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

┌─────────────┬────────────────────────────┬──────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Gene        │ Alteration                 │ Allele Freq  │ Interpretation                                                  │
├─────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ VHL         │ p.R200* (c.598C>T)         │ 68%          │ Pathogenic — Nonsense mutation; premature stop codon. Biallelic │
│             │ Exon 3 — Nonsense          │              │ inactivation confirmed via chromosome 3p allelic loss (LOH).    │
│             │                            │              │ Canonical driver of ccRCC; loss of VHL leads to HIF-1α/HIF-2α  │
│             │                            │              │ stabilization and VEGF/PDGF upregulation. Present in ~70-80%   │
│             │                            │              │ of clear cell RCC. Somatic origin; germline testing advised     │
│             │                            │              │ (patient deferred at this time).                                │
├─────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ PBRM1       │ c.2570_2571delAG           │ 44%          │ Pathogenic — Frameshift deletion; exon 15; predicted loss-of-   │
│             │ Exon 15 — Frameshift del   │              │ function with truncation. PBRM1 encodes BAF180, a chromatin      │
│             │                            │              │ remodeling subunit of the PBAF SWI/SNF complex; second most     │
│             │                            │              │ commonly mutated gene in ccRCC (~40%). Located on 3p21, subject  │
│             │                            │              │ to biallelic loss via 3p LOH. Loss may predict differential     │
│             │                            │              │ benefit from immune checkpoint inhibitors versus VEGFR TKI.     │
├─────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ BAP1        │ p.W136* (c.407G>A)         │ 52%          │ Pathogenic — Nonsense mutation; loss-of-function. BAP1 is a     │
│             │ Exon 4 — Nonsense          │              │ deubiquitinase and tumor suppressor located at 3p21.1, also     │
│             │                            │              │ subject to biallelic loss via 3p LOH. BAP1 mutations in ccRCC   │
│             │                            │              │ are associated with higher nuclear grade, sarcomatoid           │
│             │                            │              │ differentiation, and inferior oncologic outcomes. Germline BAP1 │
│             │                            │              │ mutations confer hereditary predisposition to uveal melanoma,   │
│             │                            │              │ mesothelioma, cutaneous melanoma, and ccRCC (BAP1 tumor         │
│             │                            │              │ predisposition syndrome). Germline assessment recommended.      │
├─────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ SETD2       │ p.K1740fs*2 (c.5217delA)   │ 39%          │ Pathogenic — Frameshift deletion leading to premature           │
│             │ Exon 20 — Frameshift del   │              │ termination. SETD2 encodes the sole histone H3 lysine 36        │
│             │                            │              │ trimethyltransferase (H3K36me3) in humans. Also located at      │
│             │                            │              │ 3p21, subject to biallelic inactivation via 3p LOH. Mutated in  │
│             │                            │              │ ~10-15% of ccRCC; associated with aggressive biology.           │
├─────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ TP53        │ p.R248W (c.742C>T)         │ 31%          │ Pathogenic — Hotspot missense mutation at codon 248 (CpG site); │
│             │ Exon 7 — Missense          │              │ one of the most common TP53 gain-of-function mutations.         │
│             │                            │              │ R248W confers oncogenic gain-of-function activity in addition   │
│             │                            │              │ to loss of wild-type p53 function. TP53 mutations are           │
│             │                            │              │ uncommon in classic ccRCC but enriched in tumors with           │
│             │                            │              │ sarcomatoid differentiation, as observed here. Associated with  │
│             │                            │              │ genomic instability, treatment resistance, and poor prognosis.  │
└─────────────┴────────────────────────────┴──────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SHORT VARIANT ALTERATIONS — VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE (VUS)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

┌─────────────┬────────────────────────────┬──────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Gene        │ Alteration                 │ Allele Freq  │ Interpretation                                                   │
├─────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ARID1A      │ p.S1235L (c.3704C>T)       │ 18%          │ VUS — Missense; ARID1A encodes a SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling   │
│             │ Exon 20 — Missense         │              │ subunit. Functional significance of this specific substitution  │
│             │                            │              │ uncertain; lower allele frequency may reflect subclonal origin.  │
├─────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ KDM5C       │ p.R726Q (c.2177G>A)        │ 12%          │ VUS (likely benign) — X-linked histone demethylase; uncommon   │
│             │ Exon 16 — Missense         │              │ somatic variant in RCC. Low allele frequency; likely subclonal  │
│             │                            │              │ or background polymorphism. No established clinical relevance.  │
├─────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ KMT2C       │ p.R668H (c.2003G>A)        │ 22%          │ VUS — KMT2C (MLL3) is a histone H3K4 methyltransferase;        │
│             │ Exon 8 — Missense          │              │ frequently altered across cancers. R668H not previously         │
│             │                            │              │ characterized as pathogenic; functional significance unclear.   │
└─────────────┴────────────────────────────┴──────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

┌──────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Alteration                       │ Interpretation                                                     │
├──────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Chromosome 3p LOSS               │ Loss of chromosome 3p encompassing VHL (3p25.3), PBRM1 (3p21.3),  │
│ (3p25.3–3p21 broad deletion)     │ BAP1 (3p21.1), and SETD2 (3p21.3). Represents the hallmark        │
│                                  │ genomic event in ccRCC, occurring as an early clonal initiating    │
│                                  │ event. Biallelic inactivation of all four tumor suppressors        │
│                                  │ (via somatic mutation on one allele + 3p LOH on the other)         │
│                                  │ confirmed. Consistent with the broad high-LOH profile detected.    │
├──────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ MYC GAIN                         │ Focal copy number gain at chromosome 8q24.21 (MYC locus).          │
│ (chr8q24.21, ~4–5 copies)        │ MYC amplification/gain drives increased transcriptional output      │
│                                  │ of proliferative programs; encountered in aggressive RCC and may   │
│                                  │ contribute to sarcomatoid transformation. Not a direct therapeutic │
│                                  │ target currently, but of prognostic relevance.                     │
├──────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ CDKN2A/B HOMOZYGOUS DELETION     │ Homozygous deletion of chromosome 9p21.3 encompassing both         │
│ (chr9p21.3 — homozygous del)     │ CDKN2A (encoding p16INK4a and p14ARF) and CDKN2B (encoding        │
│                                  │ p15INK4b). CDKN2A/B loss abrogates G1-S cell cycle checkpoint      │
│                                  │ control and MDM2-p53 regulatory axis (p14ARF loss). In ccRCC,      │
│                                  │ 9p21 deletion is associated with sarcomatoid differentiation,      │
│                                  │ higher grade, and shortened survival. Homozygous deletion is more  │
│                                  │ impactful than hemizygous loss; may confer synthetic lethality      │
│                                  │ vulnerabilities (CDK4/6 inhibitor rationale, investigational).     │
└──────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

No MTOR pathway alterations identified (MTOR, PTEN, TSC1, TSC2, PIK3CA — all wild-type at detected sensitivity).
No evidence of MET amplification, FGFR alteration, ALK/ROS1/RET/NTRK rearrangement, or NF2 alteration.
No amplification of VEGFR1/2/3, PDGFRA/B, or KIT.
No HER2 amplification or activating HER2 mutation detected.
No actionable gene fusions identified by RNA sequencing.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ACTIONABILITY SUMMARY — TIER CLASSIFICATION
(OncoKB / AMP-ACMG Tiering Framework)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TIER I — FDA-Approved Indications in This Tumor Type:
  • VHL biallelic loss (p.R200* + 3p LOH): Supports relevance of HIF-2α
    inhibition. Belzutifan (Welireg) is FDA-approved for VHL-mutated ccRCC
    (VHL disease-associated tumors and sporadic VHL-altered ccRCC in certain
    contexts). Relevant if prior therapies have been exhausted.
  • PD-L1 expression (CPS 15, see pathology) combined with MSS/low-TMB
    profile: consistent with immunotherapy-eligible disease (nivolumab,
    ipilimumab/nivolumab, pembrolizumab/axitinib — all FDA-approved 1L for
    advanced ccRCC regardless of PD-L1 status in this histology).

TIER II — Investigational / Emerging Biomarker Relevance:
  • BAP1 loss: Associated with sarcomatoid differentiation and poor prognosis;
    may predict enhanced benefit from immune checkpoint inhibitor combinations.
    EZH2 inhibitor activity hypothesized (H3K27 methylation dysregulation
    secondary to BAP1/ASXL loss — preclinical rationale). No approved
    indication.
  • PBRM1 loss: May identify tumors with preferential benefit from immune
    checkpoint inhibitor therapy over VEGFR TKI monotherapy (retrospective
    data from JAVELIN Renal 101, CheckMate 214 exploratory analyses).
  • CDKN2A/B homozygous deletion: CDK4/6 inhibitor sensitization rationale
    (investigational in RCC); currently no FDA-approved indication in this
    context.
  • High genomic LOH (23%): Under investigation as predictive biomarker for
    PARP inhibitor sensitivity in HRD-associated solid tumors; not established
    in ccRCC.
  • TP53 p.R248W (gain-of-function): PRIMA-1MET/APR-246 and other p53
    reactivation strategies — investigational only; no approved indication
    in RCC.
  • MYC gain: No direct targetable option currently; relevant to prognosis.

TIER III — VUS (No Current Clinical Actionability):
  • ARID1A p.S1235L, KDM5C p.R726Q, KMT2C p.R668H — as described above.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GERMLINE CONSIDERATIONS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The identified BAP1 p.W136* nonsense mutation warrants germline evaluation given
the established BAP1 Tumor Predisposition Syndrome (BAP1-TPDS), which confers
hereditary susceptibility to uveal melanoma, malignant mesothelioma, cutaneous
melanoma, and clear cell renal cell carcinoma. The allele frequency of 52% is
consistent with either a somatic mutation with LOH or a germline mutation;
distinction requires germline (blood) testing. Genetic counseling referral is
strongly recommended. Patient has been informed; germline follow-up deferred per
patient preference at time of reporting.

Note: This assay is performed on tumor tissue and is not validated for germline
variant detection. A dedicated germline assay (blood-based) is required for
definitive germline assessment.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERPRETIVE SUMMARY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Comprehensive genomic profiling of this right renal mass core needle biopsy
demonstrates a genomic landscape highly characteristic of aggressive clear cell
renal cell carcinoma with sarcomatoid differentiation. The canonical 3p loss
coupled with somatic inactivating mutations in all four major 3p tumor suppressors
(VHL, PBRM1, BAP1, SETD2) confirms biallelic inactivation of the entire 3p21
tumor suppressor cluster — a convergent genomic event seen in a subset of
high-risk ccRCC. The additional presence of TP53 p.R248W gain-of-function,
CDKN2A/B homozygous deletion, and MYC copy gain collectively describe a highly
genomically unstable tumor consistent with the observed sarcomatoid histologic
transformation (estimated 20% of tumor volume on biopsy) and high nuclear grade.

The low TMB (5 mut/Mb) and MSS status indicate that TMB-high or MSI-H directed
therapies (e.g., pembrolizumab for TMB-high or MSI-H per tissue-agnostic
approvals) are not applicable. However, the genomic profile — particularly PBRM1
loss, BAP1 loss, and high PD-L1 expression (CPS 15–18) — is consistent with
immune-inflamed biology that supports consideration of immune checkpoint
combination strategies. The absence of MTOR pathway mutations (TSC1, TSC2, MTOR
wild-type) does not favor mTOR inhibitor–based regimens as a biologically driven
choice. The absence of actionable fusions or NTRK/ALK/ROS1 alterations precludes
targeted fusion-driven therapy.

Belzutifan (HIF-2α inhibitor, Welireg) may represent a rational later-line option
given confirmed biallelic VHL loss, per its mechanism of action and growing
evidence base in VHL-altered ccRCC.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL METHODOLOGY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FoundationOne CDx is a next-generation sequencing based in vitro diagnostic device
that uses DNA extracted from FFPE tumor tissue specimens to detect substitutions,
insertion-deletions (indels), copy number alterations, and select rearrangements
in 324 genes, as well as genomic signatures including TMB and MSI. The test is
performed at Foundation Medicine, Inc. (Cambridge, MA), a CAP-accredited and
CLIA-certified laboratory. RNA sequencing was performed for fusion detection.
Genomic LOH was calculated using the GenomicLOH algorithm incorporating SNP
heterozygosity calls across the evaluable genome. Tumor content was estimated
by a board-certified pathologist on H&E-stained sections of the submitted block
prior to macrodissection.

All identified variants were reviewed and classified by a board-certified molecular
pathologist. This report is intended to supplement, and should be interpreted
alongside, the clinical and pathologic findings.
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Report reviewed and authorized by:
[Molecular Pathology Attending, Foundation Medicine]
[Board-Certified — Anatomic Pathology / Molecular Genetic Pathology]
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
